subject: Math
title: Grade K Math: Counting to 20
standard_descriptions: Count to 100 by ones and by tens
Count forward beginning from a given number
Write numbers from 0 to 20
Understand the relationship between numbers and quantities
Count to answer 'how many?' questions about as many as 20 things

subject: Math
title: Grade K Math: 2D and 3D Shapes
standard_descriptions: Describe objects in the environment using names of shapes
Correctly name shapes regardless of their orientations or overall size
Identify shapes as two-dimensional or three-dimensional

subject: Math
title: Grade 1 Math: Addition Within 20
standard_descriptions: Use addition to solve word problems within 20
Apply properties of operations as strategies to add
Relate counting to addition
Add within 20 fluently

subject: Math
title: Grade 1 Math: Subtraction Within 20
standard_descriptions: Use subtraction to solve word problems within 20
Understand subtraction as an unknown-addend problem
Subtract within 20 fluently

subject: Math
title: Grade 1 Math: Place Value — Tens and Ones
standard_descriptions: Understand that the two digits of a two-digit number represent amounts of tens and ones
Compare two two-digit numbers based on meanings of the tens and ones digits

subject: Math
title: Grade 2 Math: Addition and Subtraction Within 100
standard_descriptions: Fluently add and subtract within 100
Add up to four two-digit numbers
Add and subtract within 1000 using concrete models

subject: Math
title: Grade 2 Math: Measurement With a Ruler
standard_descriptions: Measure the length of an object by selecting and using appropriate tools
Estimate lengths using units of inches, feet, centimeters, and meters

subject: Math
title: Grade 2 Math: Introduction to Arrays
standard_descriptions: Use addition to find the total number of objects arranged in rectangular arrays with up to 5 rows and up to 5 columns

subject: Math
title: Grade 3 Math: Fractions — Parts of a Whole
standard_descriptions: Understand a fraction 1/b as the quantity formed by 1 part when a whole is partitioned into b equal parts
Understand a fraction as a number on the number line
Explain equivalence of fractions and compare fractions by reasoning about their size

subject: Math
title: Grade 3 Math: Multiplication Facts
standard_descriptions: Interpret products of whole numbers
Fluently multiply within 100

subject: Math
title: Grade 3 Math: Area and Perimeter
standard_descriptions: Recognize area as an attribute of plane figures
Measure areas by counting unit squares
Relate area to multiplication and addition
Solve problems involving perimeters of polygons

subject: Math
title: Grade 3 Math: Bar Graphs and Pictographs
standard_descriptions: Draw a scaled picture graph and a scaled bar graph to represent a data set with several categories

subject: Math
title: Grade 4 Math: Equivalent Fractions
standard_descriptions: Explain why a fraction a/b is equivalent to a fraction (n × a)/(n × b)
Compare two fractions with different numerators and different denominators

subject: Math
title: Grade 4 Math: Multi-Digit Multiplication
standard_descriptions: Multiply a whole number of up to four digits by a one-digit whole number, and multiply two two-digit numbers

subject: Math
title: Grade 4 Math: Measuring Angles
standard_descriptions: Recognize angles as geometric shapes formed wherever two rays share a common endpoint
Measure angles in whole-number degrees using a protractor

subject: Math
title: Grade 5 Math: Fraction Addition and Subtraction
standard_descriptions: Add and subtract fractions with unlike denominators
Solve word problems involving addition and subtraction of fractions

subject: Math
title: Grade 5 Math: Coordinate Plane
standard_descriptions: Use a pair of perpendicular number lines (axes) to define a coordinate system
Represent real-world and mathematical problems by graphing points in the first quadrant

subject: Math
title: Grade 6 Math: Ratios and Proportional Relationships
standard_descriptions: Understand the concept of a ratio
Understand the concept of a unit rate
Use ratio and rate reasoning to solve real-world problems

subject: Math
title: Grade 6 Math: Negative Numbers and the Number Line
standard_descriptions: Understand that positive and negative numbers describe quantities having opposite directions
Understand a rational number as a point on the number line
Understand ordering and absolute value of rational numbers

subject: Math
title: Grade 6 Math: Expressions and Equations
standard_descriptions: Write and evaluate numerical expressions involving whole-number exponents
Write, read, and evaluate expressions in which letters stand for numbers
Understand solving an equation as finding values that make it true

subject: Math
title: Grade 7 Math: Proportional Relationships
standard_descriptions: Compute unit rates associated with ratios of fractions
Recognize and represent proportional relationships between quantities

subject: Math
title: Grade 7 Math: Statistics — Sampling and Inference
standard_descriptions: Understand that statistics can be used to gain information about a population
Use data from a random sample to draw inferences about a population

subject: Math
title: Grade 8 Math: Linear Equations and Functions
standard_descriptions: Graph proportional relationships interpreting the unit rate as the slope
Understand that a function is a rule that assigns to each input exactly one output
Interpret the equation y = mx + b as defining a linear function

subject: Math
title: Grade 8 Math: Pythagorean Theorem
standard_descriptions: Explain a proof of the Pythagorean Theorem
Apply the Pythagorean Theorem to determine unknown side lengths in right triangles

subject: Science
title: Grade K Science: Seasons and Weather
standard_descriptions: Use and share observations of local weather conditions to describe patterns over time
Make observations to determine the effect of sunlight on Earth's surface

subject: Science
title: Grade K Science: Plants and What They Need
standard_descriptions: Use observations to describe patterns of what plants need to survive
Use a model to represent the relationship between the needs of different plants

subject: Science
title: Grade 2 Science: Life Cycles
standard_descriptions: Make observations of plants and animals to compare the diversity of life in different habitats
Develop models to describe that organisms have unique and diverse life cycles

subject: Science
title: Grade 2 Science: Habitats and Ecosystems
standard_descriptions: Make observations of plants and animals to compare the diversity of life in different habitats
Develop a simple model that mimics the function of an animal in dispersing seeds

subject: Science
title: Grade 3 Science: Food Chains and Energy
standard_descriptions: Construct an argument with evidence that in a particular habitat some organisms can survive well, some survive less well, and some cannot survive at all
Make a claim about the merit of a solution to a problem caused when the environment changes

subject: Science
title: Grade 3 Science: States of Matter
standard_descriptions: Plan and conduct an investigation to describe and classify different kinds of materials by their observable properties
Construct an argument with evidence that some changes caused by heating or cooling can be reversed and some cannot

subject: Science
title: Grade 4 Science: Electricity and Circuits
standard_descriptions: Make observations to provide evidence that energy can be transferred from place to place by electric currents
Apply scientific ideas to design, test, and refine a device that converts energy from one form to another

subject: Science
title: Grade 5 Science: Ecosystems — Matter and Energy
standard_descriptions: Develop a model to describe the movement of matter among plants, animals, decomposers, and the environment
Use models to describe that energy in animals' food was once energy from the sun

subject: Science
title: Grade 5 Science: Water Cycle and Earth's Systems
standard_descriptions: Develop a model using an example to describe ways the geosphere, biosphere, hydrosphere, and atmosphere interact
Describe and graph the amounts and percentages of water and fresh water in various reservoirs

subject: Science
title: Grade 5 Science: Human Body Systems
standard_descriptions: Construct an argument that plants and animals have internal and external structures that function to support survival
Use a model to describe that animals receive different types of information through their senses

subject: Science
title: Grade 6 Science: Cells — Structure and Function
standard_descriptions: Conduct an investigation to provide evidence that living things are made of cells
Develop and use a model to describe the function of a cell as a whole and ways the parts of cells contribute to the function

subject: Science
title: Grade 7 Science: Genetics — Inherited Traits
standard_descriptions: Develop and use a model to describe why structural changes to genes (mutations) may result in harmful, beneficial, or neutral effects
Develop and use a model to describe why asexual reproduction results in offspring with identical genetic information

subject: Science
title: Grade 7 Science: Earth's Layers, Rock Cycle, and Plate Tectonics
standard_descriptions: Develop a model to describe the cycling of Earth's materials and the flow of energy that drives this process
Construct an explanation based on evidence for how geoscience processes have changed Earth's surface
Identify evidence from patterns in rock formations

subject: Science
title: Grade 8 Science: Chemistry — Atoms and Molecules
standard_descriptions: Develop models to describe the atomic composition of simple molecules
Develop a model that predicts and describes changes in particle motion, temperature, and state of a pure substance

subject: Language Arts
title: Grade K ELA: Letters and Sounds
standard_descriptions: Demonstrate understanding of the organization and basic features of print
Demonstrate understanding of spoken words, syllables, and sounds
Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words

subject: Language Arts
title: Grade K ELA: Sight Words
standard_descriptions: Read common high-frequency words by sight
Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing

subject: Language Arts
title: Grade 1 ELA: Main Idea and Key Details
standard_descriptions: Identify the main topic and retell key details of a text
Ask and answer questions about key details in a text

subject: Language Arts
title: Grade 1 ELA: Story Elements
standard_descriptions: Retell stories including key details and demonstrate understanding of their central message
Describe characters, settings, and major events in a story

subject: Language Arts
title: Grade 3 ELA: Text Structure — Cause and Effect
standard_descriptions: Describe the logical connection between particular sentences and paragraphs in a text
Describe the relationship between a series of historical events, scientific ideas, or steps in technical procedures

subject: Language Arts
title: Grade 4 ELA: Figurative Language
standard_descriptions: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings
Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative language

subject: Language Arts
title: Grade 5 ELA: Opinion Writing
standard_descriptions: Write opinion pieces on topics or texts, supporting a point of view with reasons and information
Introduce a topic clearly, state an opinion, and create an organizational structure

subject: Social Studies
title: Grade 2 Social Studies: Communities — Rural, Suburban, Urban
standard_descriptions: Distinguish between urban, suburban, and rural communities
Describe the roles and responsibilities of community members

subject: Social Studies
title: Grade 3 Social Studies: Map Skills
standard_descriptions: Use geographic tools to locate and describe places
Identify the four cardinal directions and use them to describe relative locations

subject: Social Studies
title: Grade 4 Social Studies: Historical Timeline — Early America
standard_descriptions: Identify key historical events and their chronological order
Compare and contrast different perspectives on historical events

subject: Social Studies
title: Grade 4 Social Studies: Government — Branches and Roles
standard_descriptions: Describe the structure and functions of the three branches of government
Identify the roles and responsibilities of elected officials

subject: Social Studies
title: Grade 5 Social Studies: Economics — Supply, Demand, and Trade
standard_descriptions: Explain the concept of supply and demand and how it affects prices
Describe how trade benefits individuals, businesses, and countries
